text:
some thoughts and what I’m doing:

- TSMC earnings: highly bullish. two things caught my eye:

> massive capex, significant raise for 2026 and a further $100B in U.S. equipment players will benefit. great buys $AMAT $ASML $LRCX $KLAC 

> quoting the CEO: AI demand is much stronger than what we said before 

- $TSEM committing billions to silicon photonics and optical packaging. optical stack benefits such as $LITE $AAOI 

- on the other side of the table, $GOOG internal concerns over losing to OpenAI and Anthropic. rate cut odds are much higher than capex cuts imo

- Kimi K3 benchmark news, I read mixed reviews and I’m not an expert but probably overhyped like everything else with China. however, if this spooks enough people we might get a capitulation candle. 

- U.S. lawmakers asking Trump to ban Chinese memory

> simply put, fundamentals are not just good, they’re improving while the market is crashing. so basically fundamentals vs. positioning, I choose fundamentals. 

- on my trading account, I’ve already bought heavily during June lows. took some profit but I’m down -35% from ATHs. then I’ve transferred funds as I said and added significantly to my existing positions on July 7-8. 

- as of writing, now I’m down -3% on my portfolio. if this breakdowns on the charts are bear traps (tbh I’m favoring this), nothing to worry. if not and we get another material crash, I’ll once again add heavily. $SOXX chart pattern target is around 450s which is $700 for $MU and $1070 for $SNDK, give or take. however, I highly doubt that with earnings season starting next week with hyperscalers and $INTC 

> I think this is a bear trap. highly bullish developments from the sector and too obvious patterns on charts of the most crowded trades. just like the recent DXY breakout. we’ll see.

text:
There have been signs for months that the optics/photonics industry players were cognizant of InP substrate supply constraints (both resource and politically-driven) and started developing solutions around them, including GaA based VCSELs

Reason being that VCSEL supply chain is mature, well-supported and proven with massive deployment (through consumer electronic devices). 3D sensing VCSEL has existed for almost as long as the cameras on our smartphones

That's why I was confused when people dissed the slow and wide approach (shall not name who but there were a few prominent ones on X)

The biggest signal was when OCI MSA was formed earlier in March this year. The premise was:

Gen1: 4λ x 50Gbps NRZ (200Gbps/direction) 

GEN2 400Gbps/direction BiDi for up to 800Gbps per fiber

Roadmap includes scaling wavelength counts and data rates to reach 3.2Tbps per fiber and beyond (i.e. 1.6Tbps/direction BiDi) in interoperable form factors supporting pluggable, NPO/OBO and CPO

But that's not to say InP is dead. On the contrary, they are critical for long-range optics transmission

In fact, some companies like AXTI produces both InP and GaA substrates. But look into the risk factors for each company, especially the geopolitical ones because these are incredibly difficult to navigate

Lastly, on microLED. I don't believe in them because you need lots of them and fibers to work. Alignment is going to be tricky (amongst other issues). Not a scalable solution

text:
Buzzberg Daily Alpha.

We scanned every video, Substack article, tracked X research post, and Reddit discussion in Buzzberg’s feed.

The clearest conclusion: this was primarily a positioning and expectations reset in semiconductors, Korean memory, and high-beta AI, not evidence that end demand has collapsed.

Why the market fell

Crowding was the first problem. A widely shared factor snapshot showed Goldman’s high-beta momentum basket down 24% MTD, while Morgan Stanley’s tech-momentum 17-day rate of change fell 35%, its worst reading in 27 years. Multiple speakers described leverage being unwound across names already 30–50% below their highs.

Korea amplified the move. Samsung and SK Hynix had accumulated heavy retail margin exposure, while leveraged single-stock ETFs created additional daily rebalancing flows. The SK Hynix ADR also traded at a large premium while conversion constraints prevented normal arbitrage, separating $SKHY from the Korean listing. Regulatory intervention and thin holiday liquidity added further pressure.

CXMT became the narrative catalyst. Its approaching IPO revived concerns that Chinese DRAM capacity could eventually compress commodity-memory margins. Bulls argue that CXMT remains years behind in advanced DRAM and HBM; bears are looking beyond the current shortage and pricing the eventual increase in supply.

The more important signal was that good news stopped working. $TSM reported roughly 78% profit growth, raised capex to $60–64B, and guided above consensus, yet the report failed to lift semiconductors. The market is no longer questioning current AI demand. It is questioning how much future growth was already priced into the equities.

A harder, still-unconfirmed bear thesis emerged on Reddit: $AMZN selling Trainium racks, Meta selling compute, and weakness in IBM’s infrastructure business could indicate spare AI capacity. The bullish interpretation is that hyperscalers are simply monetizing constrained infrastructure rather than reducing their own deployments.

Power and permitting are another emerging constraint. New York paused large data-center projects unless developers provide their own power. That does not necessarily destroy AI capex, but it could redirect spending from chips toward onsite generation, grid equipment, and less restrictive states.

Bull case

The fundamental data remain supportive. TSMC is still unable to satisfy all leading-edge demand, ASML raised its outlook and production capacity, and memory specialists continue to expect HBM demand to outrun supply through 2028.

The less obvious signal came from CoreWeave exploring memory puts. Jukan’s interpretation is that this may confirm memory LTAs are becoming more binding: customers must hedge contracted prices instead of cancelling volumes when spot prices fall.
If hyperscalers maintain or raise capex guidance, the current decline remains a positioning reset inside an intact AI investment cycle.

Bear case

Demand can remain strong while stocks continue falling. The internet kept growing through the dot-com crash; what broke was the price investors had paid for that growth.

TSMC’s negative price reaction suggests that beats are already expected. CXMT creates a longer-term margin risk, ASML’s proposed price increases shift costs toward foundries, and data-center power constraints could delay deployments. Mary Ann Bartels sees the potential for a 30–50% semiconductor correction before the uptrend resumes.

Mark Cudmore also expects additional weakness in Korea and global chips based on poor technicals. He did not disclose a short, however, and remains positive on the AI-capex trade until hyperscalers begin cutting expenditure. In his framework, that would be the fundamental “game over” signal.
Who is actually buying

@labubu_trader  disclosed a new $SNDK  purchase while holding $MU, $SKHY/000660.KS, and $NBIS , with MAG7 still his largest exposure.

@michaelsikand  explicitly put $100K to work across six baskets, including memory ($MU/$SNDK/$EWY), photonics ($AAOI/$MRVL), AI factories ($NBIS), space ($RKLB/$ASTS), and defense.

Who is cautious

Jeong Jae-ho believes the Korean bull market remains intact, but says recent Samsung/SK Hynix buyers should reduce exposure on rebounds. Low-basis holders may keep core positions; his preferred rotation is toward Korean banks and gradual NAVER accumulation.

Mary Ann Bartels prefers Mag 7, particularly $GOOGL , and believes $META may have bottomed. She is waiting for a deeper semiconductor correction before buying that sector.

The feed’s message is clear: fundamentals have not broken, but the market has shifted from rewarding strong results to selling them. The next decisive test is hyperscaler guidance. Maintained capex supports the positioning-unwind thesis; capex cuts would turn it into a fundamental downturn.

text:
ST마이크로 실리콘 포토닉스, 300mm는 "개발 중"이 아니라 "양산 중"이다

AI 클러스터가 랙 단위에서 랙 '군집' 단위로 커지면서, 컴퓨팅 자체만큼 데이터 이동(광학 인터커넥트)이 중요해지고 있다. ST마이크로일렉트로닉스는 이미 확립한 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼(PIC100)의 산업화 — 즉 패키징·전기적 통합 역량 확보 — 를 다음 승부처로 보고 있다. 여기서 짚어야 할 건 "300mm로 가는 중"이라는 프레이밍이 아니라, "이미 300mm에서 만들고 있고, AI 수요가 그 양산 볼륨을 밀어붙이고 있다"는 사실 관계다.

📌 1. 핵심 기술 분석
구리의 한계, 광학으로 전환
짧은 거리 전기 배선용으로는 여전히 구리가 쓰이지만, 대역폭이 커지고 클러스터가 확장되면서 구리는 도달거리·전력효율·신호무결성 세 가지 측면에서 한계에 부딪히고 있다. ST 옵티컬·RF 파운드리 사업부 총괄 Sylvie Gellida는 "AI 데이터센터는 랙 내 더 많은 라우팅 능력과, 더 좁은 물리적 공간 안에서 더 큰 대역폭을 필요로 한다"고 밝혔다.
PIC100, 이미 300mm 양산 중
ST의 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 기술인 PIC100은 100-GBaud 신호로 lane당 200G를 지원한다. 핵심은 여기다 — Gellida는 "우리의 차별점은 200G/lane을 지원한다는 것 자체가 아니라, 이 기술을 300mm 웨이퍼에서 CMOS급 수율로 양산하고 있다는 것"이라고 강조했다. 실제 발언: "Today we're supplying silicon photonics wafers in Crolles, France, on 300-mm wafers." 즉 이건 "300mm로의 전환"이 아니라 "이미 검증된 300mm 플랫폼의 볼륨 확장" 스토리다.

Sicoya의 1.6T 증명
베를린 소재 Sicoya는 PIC100 기반 OSFP 모듈로 1.6T DR8(8-lane × 200G/lane) 동작을 시연했다. TIA(트랜스임피던스 증폭기)를 PIC 위에 다이 스태킹하는 방식으로, 모놀리식 통합이 아니다. Sicoya CTO Hanjo Rhee는 "다이 스태킹이 더 나은 선택"이라며, 파운드리 확장성과 기술 업데이트 속도 측면에서 유리하다고 밝혔다. 단, 이 시연은 여전히 플러거블(pluggable) 광트랜시버 영역이며 CPO(공동 패키지 광학)가 아니다.
패키징 로드맵 — TSV는 아직 개발 단계
ST는 PIC100과 BiCMOS 플랫폼용 TSV(실리콘 관통전극) 옵션을 개발 중이다. 이는 광학 소자를 프로세서/스위치 ASIC에 더 가깝게 붙이기 위한 후속 단계로, 현재 양산되는 기술이 아니라 로드맵상 개발 항목이라는 점을 구분해야 한다.

📌 2. 밸류체인
ST의 고객 구조 — 하이퍼스케일러 직접 공급 아님
ST의 직접 고객은 하이퍼스케일러 자체가 아니라 트랜시버 모듈 업체와 PIC 설계 하우스다. 이들이 ST의 실리콘 포토닉스·BiCMOS 기술로 광엔진·트랜시버·모듈을 만들고, 이것이 다시 하이퍼스케일러 생태계로 공급되는 구조다. ST는 BiCMOS(레이저 드라이버·전자 IC), STM32(모듈 제어), 패키징·테스트까지 아우르는 "원스톱 숍" 전략을 추구한다.
시장 단계 구분 — 플러거블 → NPO → CPO
Gellida에 따르면 근시일 최대 시장은 여전히 플러거블 광트랜시버(특히 scale-out 네트워크용)이며, NPO(광학을 ASIC에 더 가깝게 붙이는 중간 단계)가 다음이고, CPO는 신뢰성·서비스 가능성 이슈가 해결된 이후 더 나중에 온다. 이 순서를 생략하고 "CPO 임박"으로 서술하면 시점 오독을 유발한다.

경쟁 파운드리 구도
Sicoya CTO에 따르면 Tower Semiconductor는 상당한 포토닉스 캐파를 갖고 있지만 대부분 200mm 기반이며 이미 예약이 꽉 찬 상태다. TSMC도 포토닉스 라인업이 있지만 접근성 이슈가 있다. 300mm 기반 ST 플랫폼이 Sicoya 같은 업체에 "볼륨 확장 가능한 파운드리 파트너"로서 대안이 된다는 것이 이 기사의 경쟁 구도 핵심이다.

📌 3. 시장 변수 및 향후 관전 포인트
NPO/CPO로의 전환 속도: 서비스 모델(광커넥터 탈부착 등 조립 공정)이 아직 대량 양산 수준으로 해결되지 않음 — 이 부분 진척이 관전 포인트

ST의 300mm 캐파 확장 속도와 TSV 등 차세대 패키징 옵션의 양산 진입 시점
Marvell의 Celestial AI(2월, 35억 달러), Polariton Technologies 인수 등 광인터커넥트 관련 M&A 동향과 ST·Sicoya 생태계와의 경쟁/보완 관계

📌 4. 종합 시사점
본질은 "새로운 광학 기술의 등장"이 아니라 "이미 검증된 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼의 산업화 경쟁"이다. 기술 성능(200G/lane) 자체는 경쟁사도 도달 가능한 수준이라는 게 ST 스스로의 설명이고, 진짜 차별점은 대량 양산 캐파(300mm)와 패키징 통합 역량이라는 점이다. 이는 투자 관점에서 "누가 더 빠른 광학 소자를 만드느냐"보다 "누가 더 빨리, 더 안정적으로 대량 생산·패키징 체계를 갖추느냐"의 경쟁으로 봐야 한다는 뜻이다.

📌 5. 글로벌 핵심 종목과 투자 인사이트
직접 관련
STMicroelectronics ($STM , NYSE) — PIC100/BiCMOS 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼 공급사, 크롤레(프랑스) 팹에서 양산

Sicoya — 비상장(독일), PIC100 기반 1.6T 트랜시버 시연 파트너

경쟁/비교 대상 (비상장 또는 별도 사업부 소속으로 직접 티커 연결 어려움)

Tower Semiconductor — 200mm 중심 포토닉스 파운드리, 캐파 포화 상태

TSMC — 포토닉스 라인업 보유하나 접근성 이슈로 언급됨

국내 상장 종목 중 이번 기사에서 직접 언급되거나 확인 가능한 밸류체인 연결고리는 없다. 국내 광통신 부품·CPO 관련주(예: 광트랜시버 모듈 업체 등)와의 연관성은 이 기사만으로는 근거가 부족하므로 별도 검증 없이 엮지 않는다.

📖 용어 설명
PIC (Photonic Integrated Circuit): 광신호를 생성·변조·검출하는 회로 요소를 하나의 칩에 집적한 광집적회로
GBaud / lane: 광 신호의 심볼 전송 속도(100-GBaud) 및 한 개의 물리적 전송 경로(lane)당 데이터 속도(200G)를 뜻함. 8개 lane을 묶으면 1.6T(테라비트)가 됨
다이 스태킹 (Die Stacking) vs 모놀리식 통합: 다이 스태킹은 광소자(PIC)와 전자소자(TIA 등)를 별도로 제작한 뒤 물리적으로 쌓아 연결하는 방식. 모놀리식 통합은 애초에 하나의 칩 위에 광·전자 소자를 함께 만드는 방식으로, 신호 경로는 짧지만 개발·파운드리 유연성이 떨어짐

TSV (Through-Silicon Via, 실리콘 관통전극): 칩을 수직으로 관통해 여러 층의 칩을 전기적으로 연결하는 배선 기술. 연결 손실을 줄이고 집적도를 높이는 데 쓰임

플러거블(Pluggable) 광트랜시버: 스위치·서버 외부 포트에 꽂아 쓰는 착탈식 광통신 모듈. 현재 데이터센터 광인터커넥트의 주력 형태

NPO (Near-Packaged Optics): 광학 엔진을 ASIC/GPU 패키지에 더 가깝게(같은 기판 위 등) 배치하는 중간 단계 접근

CPO (Co-Packaged Optics, 공동 패키지 광학): 광학 엔진을 ASIC/GPU와 동일 패키지 안에 통합하는 방식. 신호 손실은 최소화되지만 신뢰성·서비스(교체·수리) 문제 해결이 아직 진행 중

클러스터가 랙 단위에서 랙 '군집' 단위로 커지면서, 컴퓨팅 자체만큼 데이터 이동(광학 인터커넥트)이 중요해지고 있다. ST마이크로일렉트로닉스는 이미 확립한 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼(PIC100)의 산업화 — 즉 https://t.co/1QGphKsntQ", "id_str": "2077885355785896335", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "ko", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 2, "retweet_count": 1, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "2062077810836123649"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzc4ODUzNTU0NDYxMDgxNjE=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": [{"from_index": 0, "richtext_types": ["Bold", "Italic"], "to_index": 44}, {"from_index": 308, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 319}, {"from_index": 1326, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 1333}, {"from_index": 1988, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2011}, {"from_index": 2253, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2262}, {"from_index": 2520, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2541}, {"from_index": 3008, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 3013}]}, "text": "ST마이크로 실리콘 포토닉스, 300mm는 "개발 중"이 아니라 "양산 중"이다 AI 클러스터가 랙 단위에서 랙 '군집' 단위로 커지면서, 컴퓨팅 자체만큼 데이터 이동(광학 인터커넥트)이 중요해지고 있다. ST마이크로일렉트로닉스는 이미 확립한 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼(PIC100)의 산업화 — 즉 패키징·전기적 통합 역량 확보 — 를 다음 승부처로 보고 있다. 여기서 짚어야 할 건 "300mm로 가는 중"이라는 프레이밍이 아니라, "이미 300mm에서 만들고 있고, AI 수요가 그 양산 볼륨을 밀어붙이고 있다"는 사실 관계다. 📌 1. 핵심 기술 분석 구리의 한계, 광학으로 전환 짧은 거리 전기 배선용으로는 여전히 구리가 쓰이지만, 대역폭이 커지고 클러스터가 확장되면서 구리는 도달거리·전력효율·신호무결성 세 가지 측면에서 한계에 부딪히고 있다. ST 옵티컬·RF 파운드리 사업부 총괄 Sylvie Gellida는 "AI 데이터센터는 랙 내 더 많은 라우팅 능력과, 더 좁은 물리적 공간 안에서 더 큰 대역폭을 필요로 한다"고 밝혔다. PIC100, 이미 300mm 양산 중 ST의 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 기술인 PIC100은 100-GBaud 신호로 lane당 200G를 지원한다. 핵심은 여기다 — Gellida는 "우리의 차별점은 200G/lane을 지원한다는 것 자체가 아니라, 이 기술을 300mm 웨이퍼에서 CMOS급 수율로 양산하고 있다는 것"이라고 강조했다. 실제 발언: "Today we're supplying silicon photonics wafers in Crolles, France, on 300-mm wafers." 즉 이건 "300mm로의 전환"이 아니라 "이미 검증된 300mm 플랫폼의 볼륨 확장" 스토리다. Sicoya의 1.6T 증명 베를린 소재 Sicoya는 PIC100 기반 OSFP 모듈로 1.6T DR8(8-lane × 200G/lane) 동작을 시연했다. TIA(트랜스임피던스 증폭기)를 PIC 위에 다이 스태킹하는 방식으로, 모놀리식 통합이 아니다. Sicoya CTO Hanjo Rhee는 "다이 스태킹이 더 나은 선택"이라며, 파운드리 확장성과 기술 업데이트 속도 측면에서 유리하다고 밝혔다. 단, 이 시연은 여전히 플러거블(pluggable) 광트랜시버 영역이며 CPO(공동 패키지 광학)가 아니다. 패키징 로드맵 — TSV는 아직 개발 단계 ST는 PIC100과 BiCMOS 플랫폼용 TSV(실리콘 관통전극) 옵션을 개발 중이다. 이는 광학 소자를 프로세서/스위치 ASIC에 더 가깝게 붙이기 위한 후속 단계로, 현재 양산되는 기술이 아니라 로드맵상 개발 항목이라는 점을 구분해야 한다. 📌 2. 밸류체인 ST의 고객 구조 — 하이퍼스케일러 직접 공급 아님 ST의 직접 고객은 하이퍼스케일러 자체가 아니라 트랜시버 모듈 업체와 PIC 설계 하우스다. 이들이 ST의 실리콘 포토닉스·BiCMOS 기술로 광엔진·트랜시버·모듈을 만들고, 이것이 다시 하이퍼스케일러 생태계로 공급되는 구조다. ST는 BiCMOS(레이저 드라이버·전자 IC), STM32(모듈 제어), 패키징·테스트까지 아우르는 "원스톱 숍" 전략을 추구한다. 시장 단계 구분 — 플러거블 → NPO → CPO Gellida에 따르면 근시일 최대 시장은 여전히 플러거블 광트랜시버(특히 scale-out 네트워크용)이며, NPO(광학을 ASIC에 더 가깝게 붙이는 중간 단계)가 다음이고, CPO는 신뢰성·서비스 가능성 이슈가 해결된 이후 더 나중에 온다. 이 순서를 생략하고 "CPO 임박"으로 서술하면 시점 오독을 유발한다. 경쟁 파운드리 구도 Sicoya CTO에 따르면 Tower Semiconductor는 상당한 포토닉스 캐파를 갖고 있지만 대부분 200mm 기반이며 이미 예약이 꽉 찬 상태다. TSMC도 포토닉스 라인업이 있지만 접근성 이슈가 있다. 300mm 기반 ST 플랫폼이 Sicoya 같은 업체에 "볼륨 확장 가능한 파운드리 파트너"로서 대안이 된다는 것이 이 기사의 경쟁 구도 핵심이다. 📌 3. 시장 변수 및 향후 관전 포인트 NPO/CPO로의 전환 속도: 서비스 모델(광커넥터 탈부착 등 조립 공정)이 아직 대량 양산 수준으로 해결되지 않음 — 이 부분 진척이 관전 포인트 ST의 300mm 캐파 확장 속도와 TSV 등 차세대 패키징 옵션의 양산 진입 시점 Marvell의 Celestial AI(2월, 35억 달러), Polariton Technologies 인수 등 광인터커넥트 관련 M&A 동향과 ST·Sicoya 생태계와의 경쟁/보완 관계 📌 4. 종합 시사점 본질은 "새로운 광학 기술의 등장"이 아니라 "이미 검증된 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼의 산업화 경쟁"이다. 기술 성능(200G/lane) 자체는 경쟁사도 도달 가능한 수준이라는 게 ST 스스로의 설명이고, 진짜 차별점은 대량 양산 캐파(300mm)와 패키징 통합 역량이라는 점이다. 이는 투자 관점에서 "누가 더 빠른 광학 소자를 만드느냐"보다 "누가 더 빨리, 더 안정적으로 대량 생산·패키징 체계를 갖추느냐"의 경쟁으로 봐야 한다는 뜻이다. 📌 5. 글로벌 핵심 종목과 투자 인사이트 직접 관련 STMicroelectronics ($STM , NYSE) — PIC100/BiCMOS 300mm 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼 공급사, 크롤레(프랑스) 팹에서 양산 Sicoya — 비상장(독일), PIC100 기반 1.6T 트랜시버 시연 파트너 경쟁/비교 대상 (비상장 또는 별도 사업부 소속으로 직접 티커 연결 어려움) Tower Semiconductor — 200mm 중심 포토닉스 파운드리, 캐파 포화 상태 TSMC — 포토닉스 라인업 보유하나 접근성 이슈로 언급됨 국내 상장 종목 중 이번 기사에서 직접 언급되거나 확인 가능한 밸류체인 연결고리는 없다. 국내 광통신 부품·CPO 관련주(예: 광트랜시버 모듈 업체 등)와의 연관성은 이 기사만으로는 근거가 부족하므로 별도 검증 없이 엮지 않는다. ※ 본 내용은 정보 제공 목적이며, 투자 판단 및 책임은 독자 본인에게 있습니다. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 📖 용어 설명 PIC (Photonic Integrated Circuit): 광신호를 생성·변조·검출하는 회로 요소를 하나의 칩에 집적한 광집적회로 GBaud / lane: 광 신호의 심볼 전송 속도(100-GBaud) 및 한 개의 물리적 전송 경로(lane)당 데이터 속도(200G)를 뜻함. 8개 lane을 묶으면 1.6T(테라비트)가 됨 다이 스태킹 (Die Stacking) vs 모놀리식 통합: 다이 스태킹은 광소자(PIC)와 전자소자(TIA 등)를 별도로 제작한 뒤 물리적으로 쌓아 연결하는 방식. 모놀리식 통합은 애초에 하나의 칩 위에 광·전자 소자를 함께 만드는 방식으로, 신호 경로는 짧지만 개발·파운드리 유연성이 떨어짐 TSV (Through-Silicon Via, 실리콘 관통전극): 칩을 수직으로 관통해 여러 층의 칩을 전기적으로 연결하는 배선 기술. 연결 손실을 줄이고 집적도를 높이는 데 쓰임 플러거블(Pluggable) 광트랜시버: 스위치·서버 외부 포트에 꽂아 쓰는 착탈식 광통신 모듈. 현재 데이터센터 광인터커넥트의 주력 형태 NPO (Near-Packaged Optics): 광학 엔진을 ASIC/GPU 패키지에 더 가깝게(같은 기판 위 등) 배치하는 중간 단계 접근 CPO (Co-Packaged Optics, 공동 패키지 광학): 광학 엔진을 ASIC/GPU와 동일 패키지 안에 통합하는 방식. 신호 손실은 최소화되지만 신뢰성·서비스(교체·수리) 문제 해결이 아직 진행 중"}}}, "rest_id": "2077885355785896335", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "637", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Almost every assumption for data centers is based upon current compute rate, heat, and energy use

Little thought is given to #photonics based computing which is 1,000 times more efficient - and is only 5 to 10 years away at scale

It feels like a Ponzi scheme where first the money goes to AI, then data centers, then orbital data centers

By the time we get orbital data centers in place in space, the cost equation will make it easier to have them on Earth, the moon or in space - whatever is most convenient without regard to location 

You'll need less environmental permitting for a future data center than you would for a refrigerator

I understand that Starship for orbital data centers is a great cash flow consideration for SpaceX 

It would certainly create the transport demand and capital needed for SpaceX to fund a Mars colony

I think we need to look behind the curtain to see what's going on 

🚀💫

text:
If a Chinese company like Moonshot can produce a frontier-level model despite restricted access to Nvidia's most advanced GPUs, investors may ask

Do future AI models really require as many top-end Nvidia chips as previously assumed?

Can software efficiency offset some of the need for massive compute?

If the answer is YES, then the market may lower its expectations for future GPU demand growth. That can hurt Nvidia and companies tied to AI infrastructure (HBM, networking, optics, photonics, testing, etc.).

text:
Citi: EML Output

> Industry Shift Toward 200G and CW: Across almost all listed companies, there is a marked transition away from 100G EML production by 2028, with a significant ramp-up in both 200G EML and CW output.

> AXT, Inc. Growth: AXT, Inc. shows a substantial increase in its 200G EML and CW output, with its 200G EML output projected to grow from 13.54 million chips in 2025 to 157.82 million chips by 2028.

> Rapid Expansion in China: Companies such as Source Photonics (Dongshan Precision) and the "Other China" category show dramatic growth in CW output. Source Photonics, for example, is projected to increase its CW output from 14.66 million chips in 2025 to 161.77 million chips by 2028.

> Sumitomo Electric: Sumitomo maintains high production levels, particularly in the 200G EML segment, which jumps from 28.93 million in 2025 to 128.58 million in 2028.

(h/t: @yulin98434)

The Morgan Stanley Tech Momentum Index just printed a 17-day rate of change of -35.9% — the worst reading in its 27-year history. Most investors are reading this as the AI trade breaking. I see a factor unwind, not a fundamental one. Momentum is one of the most successful investment factors ever discovered. Decades of research have shown that stocks with the strongest price trends tend to keep outperforming. That's why it has become the foundation of countless quantitative strategies. When everyone owns the same baskets, it doesn't take much: a quarter-end rebalance, thin summer liquidity, or a headline taken the wrong way for everyone to rush through the same exit. This time, we had all of the above, on top of one of the strongest momentum runs in years. The problem isn't momentum. The problem is that too much capital is now chasing the same momentum signals. But factor investing is very different from fundamental investing. For fundamental investors, these periods often create the best opportunities because the edge isn't in owning the broad theme. It's in understanding what each company actually does within the value chain. During times like this, we revisit every part of our coverage universe and ask a simple question: What's actually getting stronger? - Cheaper AI models may pressure AI lab economics, but they also drive higher adoption, more inference, and ultimately more hardware demand. - More memory capacity may eventually normalize memory pricing, but every new wafer requires billions of dollars of semiconductor manufacturing equipment. - AI spending isn't ending; it's broadening. Leadership is rotating from one part of the value chain to the next. The market is confusing forced selling with deteriorating fundamentals. Those are two very different things. text:
ตลาด Optical Module กำลังเข้าสู่ยุค 1.6T และ 3.2T พร้อมแรงหนุนจาก Silicon Photonics

Goldman Sachs ระบุว่า อุตสาหกรรม Optical Module กำลังเร่งเปลี่ยนผ่านไปสู่โมดูลความเร็วสูงเพื่อรองรับการเติบโตของ AI Data Center โดย 1.6T กำลังก้าวขึ้นเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของตลาด ขณะที่ 3.2T จะเริ่มเข้าสู่การผลิตจำนวนมากในปี 2027

การเปลี่ยนผ่านสู่ Optical Module ความเร็วสูง

อุตสาหกรรมกำลังเร่งเปลี่ยนจากโมดูลรุ่นเดิมไปสู่ความเร็วที่สูงขึ้น

1.6T กำลังเข้าสู่การผลิตจำนวนมาก และจะกลายเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของตลาด
3.2T คาดว่าจะเริ่มผลิตในระดับ Mass Production ตั้งแต่ปี 2027
Silicon Photonics กำลังแทนที่ EML

Goldman Sachs มองว่า Silicon Photonics (SiPh) จะเข้ามาแทนเทคโนโลยี EML (Electro-Absorption Modulated Laser) อย่างต่อเนื่อง เนื่องจากมีข้อได้เปรียบหลายด้าน ได้แก่

ใช้พลังงานต่ำกว่า
ประสิทธิภาพสูงกว่า
กระบวนการประกอบแพ็กเกจง่ายกว่า

อัตราการใช้งาน SiPh ที่คาดการณ์
800G: 60% ภายในปี 2026
1.6T: 80%
3.2T: 100%

ขณะที่สัดส่วนรายได้ของ Silicon Photonics จะเพิ่มจาก 28% ในปี 2025 เป็น 62% ภายในปี 2028

Innolight ครองตลาด 1.6T

Goldman Sachs ระบุว่า Innolight เป็นผู้นำตลาด Optical Module แบบ 1.6T Pluggable

คาดว่าจะมีส่วนแบ่งตลาด 50–60% ในปี 2026

ขณะที่รายงานจากแหล่งอื่นประเมินว่าส่วนแบ่งตลาดอาจสูงถึง 50–70%

แนวโน้ม 1.6T และ 3.2T

1.6T
คาดว่าจะกลายเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของอุตสาหกรรมภายในปี 2027
ยอดจัดส่งปี 2027 คาดอยู่ที่ 45.72 ล้านโมดูล

3.2T
หลังจาก 1.6T เริ่มแพร่หลายแล้ว
ยอดจัดส่ง 3.2T ในปี 2028 คาดว่าจะเพิ่มขึ้นเป็น 2 เท่า ของปีแรกที่เริ่มผลิตในปี 2027

เพื่อรองรับความต้องการดังกล่าว Innolight วางแผนเพิ่ม CapEx ปี 2026 ถึง 155% YoY เพื่อขยายกำลังการผลิต

การเปลี่ยนแปลงของตลาดและ ASP

ตลาดกำลังเปลี่ยนจาก Optical Module ความเร็วต่ำ ไปสู่ผลิตภัณฑ์สำหรับ AI อย่างรวดเร็ว

สัดส่วนยอดจัดส่ง
โมดูลต่ำกว่า 400G จะลดจาก 89% ในปี 2025 เหลือ 77% ในปี 2028
1.6T จะเพิ่มจาก 1% เป็น 10%
3.2T จะเพิ่มเป็น 5% ของยอดจัดส่งทั้งหมดภายในปี 2028

ด้านราคาขายเฉลี่ย (ASP)
Goldman Sachs คาดว่าราคาจะปรับลดลงตามการผลิตจำนวนมาก

1.6Tปี 2025: 840 ดอลลาร์
ปี 2028: 435 ดอลลาร์

3.2Tปี 2027: 1,023 ดอลลาร์
ปี 2028: 768 ดอลลาร์

การเปลี่ยนผ่านจาก EML ไปสู่ SiPh
ตลาดกำลังเปลี่ยนผ่านจาก EML ไปสู่ Silicon Photonics อย่างชัดเจน
สัดส่วนรายได้ของ SiPh จะเพิ่มจาก 28% ในปี 2025 เป็น 62% ในปี 2028
ส่วนแบ่งการใช้งานตามปริมาณ (Volume Penetration) จะเพิ่มจาก 6% เป็น 18% ในช่วงเวลาเดียวกัน

โอกาสของตลาด (TAM)

Goldman Sachs คาดว่ามูลค่าตลาด Optical Module ทั่วโลกจะเติบโตอย่างแข็งแกร่ง
2025: 34.2 พันล้านดอลลาร์
2027: 72.6 พันล้านดอลลาร์ (จุดสูงสุด)
2028: 69.1 พันล้านดอลลาร์

ขณะที่รายได้จากกลุ่ม 1.6T และ 3.2T ซึ่งเป็นแรงขับเคลื่อนหลักของอุตสาหกรรม จะเพิ่มจาก
3.9 พันล้านดอลลาร์ในปี 2025
เป็น 44.9 พันล้านดอลลาร์ในปี 2028

Goldman Sachs ระบุว่า อุตสาหกรรม Optical Module กำลังเร่งเปลี่ยนผ่านไปสู่โมดูลความเร็วสูงเพื่อรองรับการเติบโตของ AI Data Center โดย 1.6T กำลังก้าวขึ้นเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของตลาด ขณะที่ 3.2T จะเริ่มเข้าสู่การผลิตจำนวนมากในปี 2027

การเปลี่ยนผ่านสู่ Optical Module ความเร็วสูง

อุตสาหกรรมกำลังเร่งเปลี่ยนจากโมดูลรุ่นเดิมไปสู่ความเร็วที่สูงขึ้น

1.6T กำลังเข้าสู่การผลิตจำนวนมาก และจะกลายเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของตลาด
3.2T คาดว่าจะเริ่มผลิตในระดับ Mass Production ตั้งแต่ปี 2027
Silicon Photonics กำลังแทนที่ EML

Goldman Sachs มองว่า Silicon Photonics (SiPh) จะเข้ามาแทนเทคโนโลยี EML (Electro-Absorption Modulated Laser) อย่างต่อเนื่อง เนื่องจากมีข้อได้เปรียบหลายด้าน ได้แก่

ใช้พลังงานต่ำกว่า
ประสิทธิภาพสูงกว่า
กระบวนการประกอบแพ็กเกจง่ายกว่า

อัตราการใช้งาน SiPh ที่คาดการณ์
800G: 60% ภายในปี 2026
1.6T: 80%
3.2T: 100%

ขณะที่สัดส่วนรายได้ของ Silicon Photonics จะเพิ่มจาก 28% ในปี 2025 เป็น 62% ภายในปี 2028

Innolight ครองตลาด 1.6T

Goldman Sachs ระบุว่า Innolight เป็นผู้นำตลาด Optical Module แบบ 1.6T Pluggable

คาดว่าจะมีส่วนแบ่งตลาด 50–60% ในปี 2026

ขณะที่รายงานจากแหล่งอื่นประเมินว่าส่วนแบ่งตลาดอาจสูงถึง 50–70%

แนวโน้ม 1.6T และ 3.2T

1.6T
คาดว่าจะกลายเป็นมาตรฐานหลักของอุตสาหกรรมภายในปี 2027
ยอดจัดส่งปี 2027 คาดอยู่ที่ 45.72 ล้านโมดูล

3.2T
หลังจาก 1.6T เริ่มแพร่หลายแล้ว
ยอดจัดส่ง 3.2T ในปี 2028 คาดว่าจะเพิ่มขึ้นเป็น 2 เท่า ของปีแรกที่เริ่มผลิตในปี 2027

เพื่อรองรับความต้องการดังกล่าว Innolight วางแผนเพิ่ม CapEx ปี 2026 ถึง 155% YoY เพื่อขยายกำลังการผลิต

การเปลี่ยนแปลงของตลาดและ ASP

ตลาดกำลังเปลี่ยนจาก Optical Module ความเร็วต่ำ ไปสู่ผลิตภัณฑ์สำหรับ AI อย่างรวดเร็ว

สัดส่วนยอดจัดส่ง
โมดูลต่ำกว่า 400G จะลดจาก 89% ในปี 2025 เหลือ 77% ในปี 2028
1.6T จะเพิ่มจาก 1% เป็น 10%
3.2T จะเพิ่มเป็น 5% ของยอดจัดส่งทั้งหมดภายในปี 2028

ด้านราคาขายเฉลี่ย (ASP)
Goldman Sachs คาดว่าราคาจะปรับลดลงตามการผลิตจำนวนมาก

1.6Tปี 2025: 840 ดอลลาร์
ปี 2028: 435 ดอลลาร์

3.2Tปี 2027: 1,023 ดอลลาร์
ปี 2028: 768 ดอลลาร์

การเปลี่ยนผ่านจาก EML ไปสู่ SiPh
ตลาดกำลังเปลี่ยนผ่านจาก EML ไปสู่ Silicon Photonics อย่างชัดเจน
สัดส่วนรายได้ของ SiPh จะเพิ่มจาก 28% ในปี 2025 เป็น 62% ในปี 2028

Market Trends and Technology Migration

> Speed Evolution: The industry is aggressively migrating toward higher-speed optical modules. 1.6T modules are moving toward volume production as the next mainstream standard, while 3.2T optical modules are expected to begin volume ramp-up in 2027.

> Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Dominance: Silicon photonics is increasingly displacing traditional EML-based solutions due to lower power consumption, superior performance, and simplified packaging.

> Penetration Rates: By 2026, SiPh penetration is projected to reach 60% for 800G, 80% for 1.6T, and 100% for 3.2T products.

> Revenue Share: Silicon photonics' share of total optical module revenue is expected to surge from 28% in 2025 to 62% by 2028.

Innolight Shipment and Market Share Highlights

> Leadership: Innolight is the leading supplier of 1.6T pluggable modules. It is projected to hold a 50–60% market share of 1.6T modules in 2026. Other industry reports indicate Innolight's market share for 1.6T modules may reach as high as 50–70%.

1.6T and 3.2T Outlook:

> 1.6T: Expected to become the "absolute mainstream" by 2027, with 45.72 million units projected to be shipped that year.

> 3.2T: Following the ramp-up of 1.6T, 3.2T shipments are expected to double in 2028 compared to their initial volume ramp year in 2027.

> Manufacturing & Capacity: To support this demand, Innolight planned a 155% year-on-year increase in capital expenditure for 2026 to expand production capacity.

Market Dynamics: Volume, Revenue, and ASP

> Speed Migration (Volume Mix): The market is shifting rapidly from legacy "Below 400G" speeds toward high-speed AI-focused modules. 

While "Below 400G" accounted for 89% of shipment volume in 2025, that share is projected to decline to 77% by 2028E. 

Conversely, high-speed modules are capturing significant volume: 1.6T modules are projected to grow from 1% of volume in 2025 to 10% by 2028E, and 3.2T modules are expected to reach 5% of total shipment volume by 2028E.

> Average Selling Price (ASP) Trends: The report explicitly notes an assumed year-over-year (YoY) ASP decline for "like-for-like" products. 

For example, the ASP for 1.6T modules is expected to drop from $840 in 2025 to $435 by 2028E. 

The 3.2T module, which enters the market in 2027, has a high starting ASP of $1,023 in 2027E, which is then projected to decline to $768 by 2028E.

Technological Shift: SiPh vs. EML

> Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Adoption: The market is undergoing a significant transition from traditional EML-based materials to Silicon Photonics.

SiPh revenue penetration is expected to increase from 28% in 2025 to 62% by 2028E.

Correspondingly, the SiPh penetration rate (by volume) is projected to climb from 6% in 2025 to 18% by 2028E.

Market Opportunity (TAM)

> Total Addressable Market (TAM): The global optical module TAM is expected to grow from $34.2 billion in 2025 to a peak of $72.6 billion in 2027E, before moderating slightly to $69.1 billion in 2028E.

> High-Speed Revenue Growth: The 1.6T and 3.2T segments represent the primary growth engines, with combined revenue for these categories projected to grow from $3.9 billion in 2025 to $44.9 billion in 2028E."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 1, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 1, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2078242798571049071", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "13479", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2078261485424304227", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "238", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Just found new evidence the US government is dead serious about developing quantum technologies.

US research is funded partly through the National Science Foundation (NSF). If your grant application is approved, you receive money.

The NSF launched an initiative called NSF X-Labs two months ago. The goal of the initiative is to fund teams of researchers, engineers, and entrepreneurs to solve specific scientific challenges.

The funding available is $1,5 billion over the next decade.

The first round of X-Labs couldn’t have been more explicit. It invited proposals on two topics:

1/ Scientific instrumentation for sensing and imaging (deadline for grant applications: July 13)

2/ Quantum systems: Interconnects and integrated photonics (deadline July 20)

The initiative resonates with

1/ the $2 billion direct equity investment into nine quantum companies in May

2/ the Executive Orders on quantum in June that look to find companies capable of providing these technologies, e.g. $INFQ and $IONQ.

It’s hard not to be bullish on a technology pushed by the US government.

text:
我认为是很合理的分析

首先我们需要知道底层逻辑为什么英飞拓可以作为行业晴雨表？

我们要知道英飞拓是全球最大光模块厂商之一，它的ASP走势和出货量确实能反映整个产业链的景气度。高盛这样的机构是不会无缘无故上调108%/119%的盈利预测，那么其背后一定有channel check支撑。

所以基于此我是倾向于把英飞拓理解为光子行业的”台积电”。

而这个逻辑是台积电的资本开支指引和产能扩张节奏，是整个半导体行业景气度的先行指标；同理，英飞拓的订单结构、ASP走势和产能规划，是光子产业链景气度的先行指标。当英飞拓的盈利预测被机构上修超过100%，这不仅仅是一家公司的好消息——这是整个生态的信号弹。

800G超预期这个信号很关键。800G目前仍是主力交付速率，超预期意味着数据中心扩建节奏比市场预期快，这对LITE这类以800G为主的厂商是直接利好。

1.6T/3.2T拉高ASP的逻辑也成立，更高速率产品单价更高，即使出货量不变，收入和毛利都会改善。

高盛预期英飞拓在硅光（Silicon Photonics）模块的出货量将显著超出此前预期。这背后是三个维度的需求同步爆发：

•Scale Out（横向扩展）：更多GPU集群节点互联，需要更多光模块

•Scale Up（纵向扩展）：单节点算力密度提升，对高速互联带宽需求更高

•Scale Across（跨域扩展）：数据中心之间、城域网之间的互联需求增加
这三个维度的同步扩张，意味着光模块不再只是某一个场景的刚需，而是AI算力基础设施全面扩张的基础组件。

2. 1.6T/3.2T产品拉高ASP和利润率
光模块产品的迭代路径是：400G → 800G → 1.6T → 3.2T。更高速率的产品，单价更高，毛利率通常也更好。
1.6T和3.2T产品的加速渗透，意味着即使英飞拓的出货量增速放缓，其收入和毛利仍会因为产品结构升级而显著改善。这是一个量价齐升的逻辑——在AI Capex持续扩张的背景下，这个趋势有望延续至2028年。

3. AI Capex的持续扩张

微软、Google、Meta、亚马逊等全球四大超大规模云厂商的2025年资本开支指引，无一例外都在大幅提升。这些资金最终会流向哪里？算力芯片、服务器、网络互联设备——以及连接这一切的光模块。

英飞拓作为这条链条中最关键的环节之一，是AI Capex最直接的受益者之一。

所以我认为在方向正确 + 间接收益方面 ：我是倾向于 $soi 和 $sive的

text:
Goldman Sachs: Innolight & Optical Modules

Market Trends and Technology Migration

> Speed Evolution: The industry is aggressively migrating toward higher-speed optical modules. 1.6T modules are moving toward volume production as the next mainstream standard, while 3.2T optical modules are expected to begin volume ramp-up in 2027.

> Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Dominance: Silicon photonics is increasingly displacing traditional EML-based solutions due to lower power consumption, superior performance, and simplified packaging.

> Penetration Rates: By 2026, SiPh penetration is projected to reach 60% for 800G, 80% for 1.6T, and 100% for 3.2T products.

> Revenue Share: Silicon photonics' share of total optical module revenue is expected to surge from 28% in 2025 to 62% by 2028.

Innolight Shipment and Market Share Highlights

> Leadership: Innolight is the leading supplier of 1.6T pluggable modules. It is projected to hold a 50–60% market share of 1.6T modules in 2026. Other industry reports indicate Innolight's market share for 1.6T modules may reach as high as 50–70%.

1.6T and 3.2T Outlook:

> 1.6T: Expected to become the "absolute mainstream" by 2027, with 45.72 million units projected to be shipped that year.

> 3.2T: Following the ramp-up of 1.6T, 3.2T shipments are expected to double in 2028 compared to their initial volume ramp year in 2027.

> Manufacturing & Capacity: To support this demand, Innolight planned a 155% year-on-year increase in capital expenditure for 2026 to expand production capacity.

Market Dynamics: Volume, Revenue, and ASP

> Speed Migration (Volume Mix): The market is shifting rapidly from legacy "Below 400G" speeds toward high-speed AI-focused modules. 

While "Below 400G" accounted for 89% of shipment volume in 2025, that share is projected to decline to 77% by 2028E. 

Conversely, high-speed modules are capturing significant volume: 1.6T modules are projected to grow from 1% of volume in 2025 to 10% by 2028E, and 3.2T modules are expected to reach 5% of total shipment volume by 2028E.

> Average Selling Price (ASP) Trends: The report explicitly notes an assumed year-over-year (YoY) ASP decline for "like-for-like" products. 

For example, the ASP for 1.6T modules is expected to drop from $840 in 2025 to $435 by 2028E. 

The 3.2T module, which enters the market in 2027, has a high starting ASP of $1,023 in 2027E, which is then projected to decline to $768 by 2028E.

Technological Shift: SiPh vs. EML

> Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Adoption: The market is undergoing a significant transition from traditional EML-based materials to Silicon Photonics.

SiPh revenue penetration is expected to increase from 28% in 2025 to 62% by 2028E.

Correspondingly, the SiPh penetration rate (by volume) is projected to climb from 6% in 2025 to 18% by 2028E.

Market Opportunity (TAM)

> Total Addressable Market (TAM): The global optical module TAM is expected to grow from $34.2 billion in 2025 to a peak of $72.6 billion in 2027E, before moderating slightly to $69.1 billion in 2028E.

> High-Speed Revenue Growth: The 1.6T and 3.2T segments represent the primary growth engines, with combined revenue for these categories projected to grow from $3.9 billion in 2025 to $44.9 billion in 2028E.

text:
I want to say something about $NOK that most people aren’t saying.

The world is about to move more data than it has in its entire history combined. 

Not gradually. 

Not over decades. 

In the next four years. 

Every AI model being trained, every inference request being served, every GPU talking to another GPU inside a hyperscale data center, all of it generates traffic that has to travel somewhere. 

Through fiber. 

Through optical transceivers. 

Through routers. 

Through radio networks smart enough to handle loads that didn’t exist three years ago.

That infrastructure doesn’t build itself.

And the company quietly sitting at the center of all of it, making the optical transceivers, the IP routers, the radio access networks, and now an AI native 6G platform launched not to long ago,  is a Finnish company that most people dismissed a decade ago and haven’t looked at seriously since.

Here’s what changed.

Nokia booked €1 billion in AI and cloud orders in a single quarter. JPMorgan just raised their target from $14 to $21. Nvidia invested $1 billion at $6.01 a share, a price the stock has since blown past. AI and cloud revenue grew 49% year over year last quarter. Optical networks up 20%. Operating profit beat estimates by 12%. Guidance raised. Network Infrastructure growth forecast doubled.

Two days ago Nokia launched its AI RAN platform, a radio access network built natively for artificial intelligence that thinks and adapts in real time. Their CEO called it one of the most significant advancements in radio technology in decades.

And Nokia makes their own indium phosphide lasers in house. In Pennsylvania. In a facility just expanded 10x. Every competitor buys their lasers. Nokia makes theirs. That distinction matters more every quarter as supply chain sovereignty becomes a national priority.

Q2 earnings are July 23rd. Six days away. First report where the Nvidia partnership shows up in order books. First report where the Allentown photonics expansion starts contributing. First look at whether the €1 billion in Q1 optical orders is the floor or the ceiling.

The data has to move somewhere.

I believe $NOK Nokia is where it moves.

text:
Thanks, love reading the comments!

Goldman Sachs raised Innolight PT to 2581 RMB. Roughly 163.6% upside from current valuations.

But the largest thing is its 2026–2028 earnings est revision raised by a whopping 65%/108%/119%, based on:

-  Much higher silicon photonics module volumes
- scale out, scale up, scale across volumes
- 1.6T/3.2T lifting blended ASP + margins
- increase in AI capex

This is typically very material read through on the optical sector since:

I tend to think of Innolight as a $TSM (semi capex) type read on how the photonics landscape is doing.

Eg. Higher silicon photonics penetration means more TAM for cw lasers like $SIVE (cw) / SOI wafer demand for $SOI. ASP hikes for future gen is positive for the other optical markers too.

Think my other takeaway outside the report was Innolight stating 800g demand was growing more significantly than expected from their transcript on the 12th. 

Which in turn signals more demand for names like $AAOI to $LITE next earnings.

TLDR: GS gives high earnings projections during a time of massive corrections. 

Fundamentally, broader photonics ecosystem should be happy when it’s ER time.

text:
Morgan Stanley: Optical vs Copper

How do you think about the optical scale up opportunity in 2027, 2028, 2029, etc, and how does that relates to the Nvidia ecosystem and roadmap?

A: We believe a small scale out adoption next year, with small volumes accounting for 10-15% penetration with scale out. We do think there are some low adoption with CPO until Feynman in 2029. Small, single digit adoption of CPO in 2028 and really scaling in 2029.

You took your TAM to $73B, 4x from a year ago, talk about the drivers of that and how that compares to consensus and buy side out there?

A: Driven by CapEx data points being 2x we were expecting last year, that is one vector of growth. The other is the cluster sizes being discussed are the biggest rivers of scale up network . We have gone from 8 to 72, then from a couple 100s to 1000s in the next couple generations. This acts as a multiplier to scale up multiplier.

Talk about the exposure among Lumentum, Coherent, and Corning. Are there nuances between who is more exposed, less exposed?

A: For CPO within scale out, it is generally positive for Lumentum. Doesn't necessarily changed a whole lot for Corning. With Coherent, they have to balance it out with transceiver business they have. For Lumentum it is the most positive because of the one customer they have on the transceiver side being google and they are not going to be adopting CPO. For CPO within scale up, it is opportunity for all 3. This is where copper gets replaced with fiber and lit up by lasers and optical engines made by Coherent an Lumentum. In terms of overall exposure, Lumentum has a bigger catalyst because of larger AI/DC revenue. It would be incremental for both Coherent and lumentum. Lumentum #1, Coherent or Corning #2.

Tell me about the Corning Glass Bridge announcement that got the stock excited?

A: This is not a new announcement. It was a paper they released in 2025 and a presentation they did that refocused the market on this technology. It is already part of the $10B photonics target the company discussed couple of months ago.

In terms of catalyst for optical space, OCP (Open Compute Project) coming in October has gone popular. What are you expecting to hear from this?

A: We think this will be a catalyst because it provides more exposure into hyperscaler behavior. OCP will give us a chance to see hyperscalers roadmap, it will focus on CPO, NPO adoption and the vendors involved.

Copper is still the story for 2026 and 2027. We have seen this continued enthusiasm for ASICs. How is copper well positioned for this dynamic? Who are the well positioned beneficiaries?

A: Copper life can be extended with use of retimers and better SerDes. Copper has a lot of runway before switching to optics. We will likely see a hybrid environment before going to 100% optics. A large debate in scale up is what fabric and protocol will become dominant other than NVLINK as we get into new XPUs and alternatives to Nvidia GPUs ramping. A big debate here is between UALink and ethernet. Ethernet is dependent on Broadcom's new ASICs ramping in 2H2027 into 2028, we think Broadcom will have opportunity to bundle XPU solutions with networking portfolio and are very well positioned in ethernet. On UALink, this is high optionality for Marvell and Astera Labs. Astera has the scale up switch for Trainium 3, this is a PCIe based solution. We think customers will want to move to UA Link eventually.

You consider Keysight as an agnostic winner in R&D, regardless of the copper vs optical timeline. Explain your thesis and why do you see it pulling ahead?

A: Keysight really ways in multiple ways when it comes to AI. First, it benefits from different types of architectures being discussed which all need to be tested with their equipment in labs. Second, when you move to higher speeds (400g laser vs 200g), it requires more complicated testing. Third, when architectures change frequently, like every 2 years, that boosts their business. The variety of architectures being tested matters.

AI Infrastructure Stack Leaders – July 2026 Update
Ran the full framework across the 6 key layers of the AI buildout. NOT investment advice, DYOR. Here’s the current picture:
Layer Scores (Leadership Strength + Conviction):

Power Delivery & Critical Power: 86 / 70 → Top Layer
Advanced Cooling & Thermal Management: 84 / 68 → Top Layer
Compute Accelerators & Custom Silicon: 89 / 73
High-Performance Networking & Fabric: 83 / 68
Optics & Photonics / CPO: 67 / 54
Data Center Facilities / REITs: 54 / 43 → Weakest layer

Gross Layer Stance: Neutral to Increase Power and Cooling currently lead on fundamentals. Compute remains strong but more expensive. Most layers have seen price weakness recently, which improves entry points but reflects market caution.

text:
OPTICAL INTERCONNECTS AND SILICON PHOTONICS

SILICON-PHOTONICS BURN-IN HAS MOVED FROM QUALIFICATION INTO AUTOMATED HIGH-VOLUME PRODUCTION (READ-THROUGH 6)

Affected companies: Coherent Corp. (COHR: United States), Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE: United States), Broadcom Inc. (AVGO: United States), and Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL: United States) are positively affected. Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO: United States) and Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET: United States) receive a positive demand read-through, partially offset by potentially higher optical-component and qualification costs.

Directional impact and magnitude: Moderate positive near-term and high positive longer-duration for optical-component and optical-connectivity suppliers. Moderate positive for data-center networking vendors because the call supports continued high-speed optical deployment, although it does not identify the networking customer or establish a specific vendor design win.

Call evidence: Silicon photonics and optical-device applications represented approximately 20% of Aehr’s FY2026 revenue. The lead customer placed follow-on production orders and is operating fully automated wafer-level burn-in integrated with automated wafer handling and automated guided vehicles. A new “global leader in networking products and solutions” progressed from initial engagement in approximately November to orders for 2 9-wafer FOX test cells and 2 FOX-NP systems, with additional systems forecast during calendar 2026. Management expects silicon photonics to represent approximately 15%-20% of a much larger FY2027 revenue base.

Transmission mechanism: Burn-in equipment is generally not installed at production scale unless customers anticipate sufficient optical-device volume and reliability requirements to justify the capital and process cost. The move to hands-free automated wafer-level burn-in indicates that silicon-photonic devices are being manufactured in sustained production volumes rather than limited qualification lots.

Coherent and Lumentum benefit from growth in optical components, lasers, photonic devices, and high-speed transceiver content. Broadcom and Marvell benefit from optical DSPs, SerDes, switch silicon, custom connectivity silicon, and optical-interconnect architectures. Cisco and Arista benefit from hyperscale network upgrades requiring more 800G, 1.6T, and future higher-speed optical links.

The call is strongest as evidence for high-volume optical interconnect and silicon-photonic device production. It should not be interpreted as definitive evidence that co-packaged optics has already reached broad mass deployment. The disclosed applications include optical transceivers, optical I/O, chip-to-chip links, and data-center interconnects, which can be addressed through several packaging architectures.

The requirement for production burn-in also creates differentiation among optical suppliers. Vendors with superior process control, yields, reliability data, and capacity should gain share because hyperscalers increasingly require early-life-failure screening. Vendors with weaker yields face higher scrap and test costs. This should favor scaled suppliers over less mature optical manufacturers.

Near-term trading catalyst: Additional production-system orders from the new networking customer would be a strong confirmation that the current optical deployment cycle is expanding rather than reflecting a single-customer program.

Long-duration fundamental shift: Optical content per accelerator and per switch should continue rising as electrical interconnect distances, bandwidth requirements, and power constraints become more difficult. Reliability screening becomes a necessary enabling cost of that transition rather than an optional quality step.

GEOGRAPHIC SUPPLY CHAIN

AI SEMICONDUCTOR PRODUCTION IS BECOMING MORE CONCENTRATED IN TAIWAN AT THE TEST AND BURN-IN STAGE (READ-THROUGH 7)

Affected companies: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM: Taiwan) and ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. (ASX: Taiwan) receive a positive operating-activity read-through. NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA: United States), Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD: United States), Broadcom Inc. (AVGO: United States), Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL: United States), Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL: United States), https://t.co/SpqvHNV5fi, Inc. (AMZN: United States), Microsoft Corporation (MSFT: United States), and Meta Platforms, Inc. (META: United States) face a negative supply-chain concentration read-through.

Directional impact and magnitude: Moderate positive for Taiwanese semiconductor activity and ecosystem density. Minimal immediate negative earnings impact for US AI companies, but high potential magnitude under a geopolitical, logistics, power, water, or natural-disaster disruption scenario.

Call evidence: The $41 million hyperscale Sonoma order is expected to be delivered to the customer’s OSAT in Taiwan. The new AI processor benchmark customer wants pilot-production validation at a semiconductor contract manufacturer in Taiwan. Aehr expanded its Hsinchu office to increase local sales and customer-support personnel. The company also won its 1st Taiwanese silicon-carbide customer.

Transmission mechanism: Advanced AI processors already depend heavily on Taiwan for leading-edge wafer fabrication and advanced packaging. The call indicates that specialized reliability screening, production burn-in, process validation, and local engineering support are also concentrating in the same geography. This creates greater operational efficiency because foundry, OSAT, equipment, engineering, and customers are geographically proximate, but it also increases correlated supply-chain risk.

TSMC and ASE benefit from ecosystem clustering, faster customer qualification, more production programs, and additional process steps being localized in Taiwan. Local presence can shorten installation, debugging, yield-correlation, and ramp schedules.

The negative portfolio implication is that diversification at the chip-design level does not necessarily diversify the manufacturing chain. Merchant GPUs, custom hyperscaler accelerators, CPUs, network processors, optical devices, and advanced packages can remain dependent on overlapping Taiwanese infrastructure. A disruption could therefore affect multiple ostensibly diversified AI exposures simultaneously.

Near-term trading catalyst: This is not a conventional near-term catalyst absent a geopolitical or operational event. The immediate positive is continued capacity utilization and customer activity in Taiwan.

Long-duration fundamental shift: Taiwan remains the central integration point for advanced foundry, packaging, burn-in, and production engineering. Portfolio-level correlation risk is therefore higher than would be inferred from company-specific revenue diversification alone.

POWER SEMICONDUCTORS: SILICON CARBIDE

SILICON-CARBIDE DEMAND IS SHOWING A CREDIBLE BUT NARROW RECOVERY SIGNAL (READ-THROUGH 8)

Affected companies: onsemi (ON: United States), STMicroelectronics N.V. (STM: Switzerland), Infineon Technologies AG (IFX: Germany), and Wolfspeed, Inc. (WOLF: United States) receive a positive demand read-through.

Directional impact and magnitude: Moderate positive near-term for silicon-carbide sentiment, particularly for companies with meaningful automotive exposure. Low positive for the earnings of diversified large-cap suppliers until broader order, utilization, and pricing evidence emerges. Potentially larger positive for Wolfspeed’s demand narrative, although company-specific manufacturing, financing, and execution risks remain separate.

Call evidence: Aehr received approximately $8 million of new silicon-carbide WaferPak orders in roughly 1 month. These included expanded production orders from the lead silicon-carbide customer and a direct order from “one of the largest automotive companies in the world” for multiple WaferPaks to qualify supplier devices for a new generation of electric vehicles. Management stated that customer forecasts are more specific than 1 year earlier and that portions of the installed base are operating near full capacity.

Transmission mechanism: WaferPak contactor orders are closely associated with device-specific qualification and production capacity. Expanded orders from an existing production customer suggest rising utilization rather than only initial engineering interest. The direct automotive-OEM purchase is particularly important because it indicates that the OEM is taking greater control over supplier qualification and reliability validation.

OEM-directed qualification can create downstream pull-through. Suppliers seeking approval for a new electric-vehicle platform may need to demonstrate reliability using the OEM’s specified screening method. Approved suppliers may then require additional wafer-level burn-in capacity and consumables for production. This favors silicon-carbide manufacturers with established automotive quality systems, reliable yields, and the ability to support stringent qualification requirements.

The signal is not sufficient to conclude that the entire silicon-carbide cycle has recovered. The $8 million order is meaningful for Aehr but small relative to global silicon-carbide revenue, and a portion may be concentrated in 1 lead customer. Automotive inventory, EV adoption, pricing pressure, and Chinese capacity remain material sector variables.

Near-term trading catalyst: Additional system orders following the WaferPak purchases would demonstrate that qualification demand is converting into production-capacity expansion. Rising utilization at Aehr’s installed base is an earlier indicator than reported silicon-carbide revenue, but it requires corroboration from the semiconductor suppliers.

Long-duration fundamental shift: Large automotive OEMs appear increasingly willing to impose direct reliability requirements on silicon-carbide suppliers. This should favor scaled, qualified incumbents and make process control and burn-in capability more important competitive variables.

POWER SEMICONDUCTORS: GALLIUM NITRIDE

MANDATORY PRODUCTION BURN-IN IS BOTH A COST HEADWIND AND AN ADOPTION ENABLER FOR GAN (READ-THROUGH 9)

Affected companies: Infineon Technologies AG (IFX: Germany) and Power Integrations, Inc. (POWI: United States) receive a positive relative read-through. Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS: United States) receives a mixed read-through consisting of positive end-market validation but potentially greater near-term qualification, test, and manufacturing-cost pressure.

Directional impact and magnitude: Low near-term trading impact because no large GaN production-system order was disclosed. Moderate positive longer-duration for scaled GaN suppliers capable of absorbing burn-in costs and maintaining high yields. Negative near-term margin implications for suppliers with immature processes, weak yields, limited balance sheets, or insufficient pricing power.

Call evidence: Aehr completed more than 12 GaN WaferPak designs that are being sampled by prospective customers. Management stated that “many if not all” are expected to move into volume production and that every major application evaluated to date appears to require production burn-in to meet reliability requirements. Aehr also completed what it described as the 1st 300-millimeter GaN wafer-level burn-in solution.

Transmission mechanism: GaN devices offer switching-efficiency, frequency, size, and power-density advantages, but latent defect mechanisms and reliability concerns have limited adoption in automotive, industrial, and data-center applications. Mandatory production burn-in increases test cost, processing time, capital requirements, and scrap. This is a near-term economic burden for semiconductor suppliers.

The same burn-in capability can enable adoption by screening early failures before devices are shipped into high-value systems. Automotive and data-center customers are unlikely to adopt GaN broadly without reliable quality data and production screening. The availability of a 300-millimeter wafer-level solution also supports scale because larger wafers can reduce cost per die and make high-volume screening more economical.

Infineon benefits from manufacturing scale, application breadth, automotive qualification capabilities, and the ability to spread reliability investment across a large revenue base. Power Integrations benefits if integrated GaN solutions gain adoption in power supplies and high-efficiency conversion applications where reliability can be demonstrated. Navitas benefits from broader customer acceptance of GaN, but mandatory burn-in may place proportionally greater pressure on gross margin, working capital, and outsourced-manufacturing economics.

Near-term trading catalyst: Production FOX-system orders associated with the sampled GaN WaferPaks would provide the 1st strong evidence that qualification activity is converting into material volume manufacturing.

Long-duration fundamental shift: Burn-in should be viewed as an enabling infrastructure layer for GaN commercialization. The requirement raises barriers to entry and should favor suppliers with better process control, higher yields, stronger customer qualification, and sufficient scale to absorb additional test cost.

PORTFOLIO SYNTHESIS

The highest-conviction near-term positive read-throughs are the production validation of hyperscaler custom AI silicon, the continued increase in AI device power density, the transition of silicon-photonics burn-in into automated volume production, and the narrow but tangible improvement in silicon-carbide activity. The most attractive longer-duration beneficiaries are Broadcom and Marvell through custom silicon and connectivity, Vertiv and Eaton through rising power and thermal intensity, Synopsys and Cadence through design-for-test complexity, and TSMC and ASE through continued concentration of advanced AI manufacturing in Taiwan. The clearest longer-duration relative negatives are incremental hyperscaler share pressure on NVIDIA and AMD from custom accelerators and the migration of selected burn-in spending away from conventional package-level and system-level test platforms supplied by Advantest, Teradyne, and Cohu. The call does not imply an absolute contraction in merchant GPUs or conventional semiconductor test; it indicates that the composition of AI semiconductor spending is broadening and that a greater percentage of value is moving into custom silicon, advanced packaging, pre-package reliability screening, optical connectivity, and power infrastructure.

Reliability screening becomes a necessary enabling cost of that transition rather than an optional quality step. GEOGRAPHIC SUPPLY CHAIN AI SEMICONDUCTOR PRODUCTION IS BECOMING MORE CONCENTRATED IN TAIWAN AT THE TEST AND BURN-IN STAGE (READ-THROUGH 7) Affected companies: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM: Taiwan) and ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. (ASX: Taiwan) receive a positive operating-activity read-through. NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA: United States), Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD: United States), Broadcom Inc. (AVGO: United States), Marvell Technology, Inc. (MRVL: United States), Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL: United States), https://t.co/SpqvHNV5fi, Inc. (AMZN: United States), Microsoft Corporation (MSFT: United States), and Meta Platforms, Inc. (META: United States) face a negative supply-chain concentration read-through. Directional impact and magnitude: Moderate positive for Taiwanese semiconductor activity and ecosystem density. Minimal immediate negative earnings impact for US AI companies, but high potential magnitude under a geopolitical, logistics, power, water, or natural-disaster disruption scenario. Call evidence: The $41 million hyperscale Sonoma order is expected to be delivered to the customer’s OSAT in Taiwan. The new AI processor benchmark customer wants pilot-production validation at a semiconductor contract manufacturer in Taiwan. Aehr expanded its Hsinchu office to increase local sales and customer-support personnel. The company also won its 1st Taiwanese silicon-carbide customer. Transmission mechanism: Advanced AI processors already depend heavily on Taiwan for leading-edge wafer fabrication and advanced packaging. The call indicates that specialized reliability screening, production burn-in, process validation, and local engineering support are also concentrating in the same geography. This creates greater operational efficiency because foundry, OSAT, equipment, engineering, and customers are geographically proximate, but it also increases correlated supply-chain risk. TSMC and ASE benefit from ecosystem clustering, faster customer qualification, more production programs, and additional process steps being localized in Taiwan. Local presence can shorten installation, debugging, yield-correlation, and ramp schedules. The negative portfolio implication is that diversification at the chip-design level does not necessarily diversify the manufacturing chain. Merchant GPUs, custom hyperscaler accelerators, CPUs, network processors, optical devices, and advanced packages can remain dependent on overlapping Taiwanese infrastructure. A disruption could therefore affect multiple ostensibly diversified AI exposures simultaneously. Near-term trading catalyst: This is not a conventional near-term catalyst absent a geopolitical or operational event. The immediate positive is continued capacity utilization and customer activity in Taiwan. Long-duration fundamental shift: Taiwan remains the central integration point for advanced foundry, packaging, burn-in, and production engineering. Portfolio-level correlation risk is therefore higher than would be inferred from company-specific revenue diversification alone. POWER SEMICONDUCTORS: SILICON CARBIDE SILICON-CARBIDE DEMAND IS SHOWING A CREDIBLE BUT NARROW RECOVERY SIGNAL (READ-THROUGH 8) Affected companies: onsemi (ON: United States), STMicroelectronics N.V. (STM: Switzerland), Infineon Technologies AG (IFX: Germany), and Wolfspeed, Inc. (WOLF: United States) receive a positive demand read-through. Directional impact and magnitude: Moderate positive near-term for silicon-carbide sentiment, particularly for companies with meaningful automotive exposure. Low positive for the earnings of diversified large-cap suppliers until broader order, utilization, and pricing evidence emerges. Potentially larger positive for Wolfspeed’s demand narrative, although company-specific manufacturing, financing, and execution risks remain separate. Call evidence: Aehr received approximately $8 million of new silicon-carbide WaferPak orders in roughly 1 month. These included expanded production orders from the lead silicon-carbide customer and a direct order from “one of the largest automotive companies in the world” for multiple WaferPaks to qualify supplier devices for a new generation of electric vehicles. Management stated that customer forecasts are more specific than 1 year earlier and that portions of the installed base are operating near full capacity. Transmission mechanism: WaferPak contactor orders are closely associated with device-specific qualification and production capacity. Expanded orders from an existing production customer suggest rising utilization rather than only initial engineering interest. The direct automotive-OEM purchase is particularly important because it indicates that the OEM is taking greater control over supplier qualification and reliability validation. OEM-directed qualification can create downstream pull-through. Suppliers seeking approval for a new electric-vehicle platform may need to demonstrate reliability using the OEM’s specified screening method. Approved suppliers may then require additional wafer-level burn-in capacity and consumables for production. This favors silicon-carbide manufacturers with established automotive quality systems, reliable yields, and the ability to support stringent qualification requirements. The signal is not sufficient to conclude that the entire silicon-carbide cycle has recovered. The $8 million order is meaningful for Aehr but small relative to global silicon-carbide revenue, and a portion may be concentrated in 1 lead customer. Automotive inventory, EV adoption, pricing pressure, and Chinese capacity remain material sector variables. Near-term trading catalyst: Additional system orders following the WaferPak purchases would demonstrate that qualification demand is converting into production-capacity expansion. Rising utilization at Aehr’s installed base is an earlier indicator than reported silicon-carbide revenue, but it requires corroboration from the semiconductor suppliers. Long-duration fundamental shift: Large automotive OEMs appear increasingly willing to impose direct reliability requirements on silicon-carbide suppliers. This should favor scaled, qualified incumbents and make process control and burn-in capability more important competitive variables. POWER SEMICONDUCTORS: GALLIUM NITRIDE MANDATORY PRODUCTION BURN-IN IS BOTH A COST HEADWIND AND AN ADOPTION ENABLER FOR GAN (READ-THROUGH 9) Affected companies: Infineon Technologies AG (IFX: Germany) and Power Integrations, Inc. (POWI: United States) receive a positive relative read-through. Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NVTS: United States) receives a mixed read-through consisting of positive end-market validation but potentially greater near-term qualification, test, and manufacturing-cost pressure. Directional impact and magnitude: Low near-term trading impact because no large GaN production-system order was disclosed. Moderate positive longer-duration for scaled GaN suppliers capable of absorbing burn-in costs and maintaining high yields. Negative near-term margin implications for suppliers with immature processes, weak yields, limited balance sheets, or insufficient pricing power. Call evidence: Aehr completed more than 12 GaN WaferPak designs that are being sampled by prospective customers. Management stated that “many if not all” are expected to move into volume production and that every major application evaluated to date appears to require production burn-in to meet reliability requirements. Aehr also completed what it described as the 1st 300-millimeter GaN wafer-level burn-in solution. Transmission mechanism: GaN devices offer switching-efficiency, frequency, size, and power-density advantages, but latent defect mechanisms and reliability concerns have limited adoption in automotive, industrial, and data-center applications. Mandatory production burn-in increases test cost, processing time, capital requirements, and scrap. This is a near-term economic burden for semiconductor suppliers. The same burn-in capability can enable adoption by screening early failures before devices are shipped into high-value systems. Automotive and data-center customers are unlikely to adopt GaN broadly without reliable quality data and production screening. The availability of a 300-millimeter wafer-level solution also supports scale because larger wafers can reduce cost per die and make high-volume screening more economical. Infineon benefits from manufacturing scale, application breadth, automotive qualification capabilities, and the ability to spread reliability investment across a large revenue base. Power Integrations benefits if integrated GaN solutions gain adoption in power supplies and high-efficiency conversion applications where reliability can be demonstrated. Navitas benefits from broader customer acceptance of GaN, but mandatory burn-in may place proportionally greater pressure on gross margin, working capital, and outsourced-manufacturing economics. Near-term trading catalyst: Production FOX-system orders associated with the sampled GaN WaferPaks would provide the 1st strong evidence that qualification activity is converting into material volume manufacturing. Long-duration fundamental shift: Burn-in should be viewed as an enabling infrastructure layer for GaN commercialization. The requirement raises barriers to entry and should favor suppliers with better process control, higher yields, stronger customer qualification, and sufficient scale to absorb additional test cost. PORTFOLIO SYNTHESIS The highest-conviction near-term positive read-throughs are the production validation of hyperscaler custom AI silicon, the continued increase in AI device power density, the transition of silicon-photonics burn-in into automated volume production, and the narrow but tangible improvement in silicon-carbide activity. The most attractive longer-duration beneficiaries are Broadcom and Marvell through custom silicon and connectivity, Vertiv and Eaton through rising power and thermal intensity, Synopsys and Cadence through design-for-test complexity, and TSMC and ASE through continued concentration of advanced AI manufacturing in Taiwan. The clearest longer-duration relative negatives are incremental hyperscaler share pressure on NVIDIA and AMD from custom accelerators and the migration of selected burn-in spending away from conventional package-level and system-level test platforms supplied by Advantest, Teradyne, and Cohu. The call does not imply an absolute contraction in merchant GPUs or conventional semiconductor test; it indicates that the composition of AI semiconductor spending is broadening and that a greater percentage of value is moving into custom silicon, advanced packaging, pre-package reliability screening, optical connectivity, and power infrastructure."}}}, "rest_id": "2077175993631211883", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1812", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}
